channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

item.title: Crunchyroll Unveils Anime Awards Nominees: Cast Your Vote Before It’s Too Late
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Voting is open! See the 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards nominees, find out how to cast your vote, and get the live ceremony streaming schedule.
item.content:encoded:
Crunchyroll announces its Anime Awards nominees

The Crunchyroll Anime Awards are right around the corner, so now’s the time to make your voice heard and vote for your favorite titles. Crunchyroll just unveiled its 2026 nominees, including longtime favorites like “DAN DA DAN,” “My Hero Academia,” and “The Apothecary Diaries” as well as popular newcomers “Gachiakuta” and “The Summer Hikaru Died.” Want to get your vote in before the April 15 deadline? Read on to find out how, and then tune back in on May 23 to watch the ceremony.

How to vote for the Crunchyroll Anime Awards

You have two options for voting in the Crunchyroll Anime Awards: Crunchyroll and MyAnimeList. You have to sign in with your Crunchyroll or MAL credentials, and you can vote once per day. You may not have access to all 32 categories depending on which language dubs you’ve watched with your account. Personally, I found the categories easy to decide until I got to the character awards. You’re telling me I have to choose between Enjin, Shisui, and Turbo Granny for Best Supporting Character?! That’s just cruel. Voting closes on April 15, so get your votes in now!

Crunchyroll Anime Awards nominees

The Crunchyroll Anime Awards honor a lot of different types of anime across 32 categories, even anime that doesn’t air on Crunchyroll itself. This year’s nominees include a lot of stellar titles. Here’s a glimpse at some of the anime you can vote for before April 15.

  • “DAN DA DAN”
  • “Gachiakuta”
  • “My Hero Academia”
  • “Takopi’s Original Sin”
  • “The Apothecary Diaries”
  • “The Summer Hikaru Died”
  • “ZENSHU”
  • “DIGIMON BEATBREAK”
  • “Kaiju No. 8”
  • “SPY x FAMILY”
  • “SAKAMOTO DAYS”
  • “RANMA ½”
  • “My Dress-Up Darling”

You can check Crunchyroll’s website for the full list of nominees in all 32 categories.

How to watch the Crunchyroll Anime Awards

This year’s Crunchyroll Anime Awards will air on Saturday, May 23 at 5:00 p.m. JST, which is 4:00 a.m. EDT for us Americans. So while most of us won’t be able to catch the ceremony live, it’ll still be available on demand on Crunchyroll later in the day.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Crunchyroll Spring 2026 Lineup
  • Spring 2026 Anime Simulcast Schedule
  • ‘Witch Hat Atelier’ Gets An Anime Adaptation

item.title: The 9 Spiciest ‘Euphoria’ Episodes: From Hazing Violence to That Infamous Bathroom Scene
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Euphoria Season 3 is almost here! We’ve researched the 9 spiciest Euphoria episodes so you don’t have to. Relive every shocking HBO moment before April 12.
item.content:encoded:
You can call “Euphoria” brilliant or contrived, but you definitely can’t call it chill. The HBO series, about a group of teenaged friends in a fictional California suburb, is jam-packed with violent boyfriends, creepy-as-hell drug dealers, wanton sexual activity, and some seriously agonizing depictions of drug addiction. Created by Sam Levinson, “Euphoria” also has great music, smart writing, and a killer cast that includes era-defining stars Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney. But let’s be real, what really gets people talking are the show’s spiciest and most shocking moments, of which there are many. With Season 3 premiering on April 12, here’s a guide to the HBO series’ most extra, outrageous, over-the-top, unsettling episodes.

“Pilot” (Season 1, Episode 1)

”Euphoria” jumps right into the shock and awe as the very first episode features not one, but two sex scenes, both involving adults hooking up with minors. Certainly the most outrageous of the two is when freewheeling transgender teen Jules Vaughn goes to a motel to hook up with Cal Jacobs, a full-grown man and father of Jules’ evil classmate Nate. Going by the ultra-cringe handle DominantDaddy on the dating app where they meet, Cal lays the groundwork for a major plotline when he films their tryst without her consent.

“Stuntin’ Like My Daddy” (Season 1, Episode 2)

After Maddy gets down with an older boy named Tyler in a swimming pool during a party in the pilot, Maddy’s ex-boyfriend Nate hunts down Tyler at his apartment, brutally interrogates him, and beats him senseless. The violence itself is shocking enough, but it’s extra creepy when Nate calmly cleans himself up afterwards and looks at himself in the mirror while Tyler silently whimpers on the floor.

“Shook Ones Pt. II” (Season 1, Episode 4)

Varsity quarterback Nate is by far the scariest and most abusive student at East Highland High School, and if you aren’t already convinced of his dark side, then just consider what he does in “Shook Ones Pt. II.” As one of the plotlines of this acclaimed episode, which revolves around a trip to a carnival, Nate catfishes Jules and coerces her into staying quiet about her relationship with Cal by threatening to report nude photos of her and get her prosecuted for “child pornography.”

“The Next Episode” (Season 1, Episode 6)

College-aged football star Chris McKay is one of the kindest characters on the entire show, but he suffers serious psychological damage in “The Next Episode” when he gets assaulted by a gang of masked frat brothers in front of his girlfriend at a party. The disturbing hazing scene generated a lot of discussion online because the editing makes the attack look like a rape, although Algee Smith (who plays McKay) said later that it was “fake humping.”

“And Salt the Earth Behind You” (Season 1, Episode 8)

A heartbreaking rift between Rue Bennett (played by Zendaya) and her beloved Jules leads to a whimsically haunting relapse when Rue goes home and snorts a line of narcotic powder. Over the off-kilter R&B of Labrinth and Zendaya’s R&B jam “All for Us,” Zendaya enacts a kind of funeral procession as she stumbles outside into a street crowded with dancers and gets held aloft in a reflection of her journey through grief and addiction.

“Trying to Get to Heaven Before They Close the Door” (Season 2, Episode 1)

The unforgettable “I have to pee!” scene goes down when Maddy pounds on the door of the bathroom at yet another depraved dance party full of underage kids. Inside the bathroom that Maddy is trying to get into, her best friend Cassie Howard (played by Sydney Sweeney) is freaking out because she’s right in the middle of getting it on with Maddy’s ex, the infamous Nate. This episode also marks the harrowing introduction of Laurie, a dead-eyed schoolteacher turned drug dealer.

“Ruminations: Big and Little Bullys” (Season 2, Episode 3)

It’s more touching than spicy, but “Ruminations: Big and Little Bullys” is worth a mention for showing Cal’s origin story as a teenage wrestling champion who discovers his sexuality with his best friend Derek when they share a dance and a kiss at a local gay bar. This being “Euphoria” and all, Cal’s dreams of ever having a functional relationship are then crushed when he finds out that his girlfriend (and future wife) Martha is pregnant.

“Stand Still Like a Hummingbird” (Season 2, Episode 5)

In what’s widely regarded as the most painful moment in “Euphoria” history, Rue delivers a monumentally hurtful drug-addict rant to her sister, her mother, and Jules after a suitcase full of drugs that she got from Laurie goes missing. It’s truly one of the most powerful depictions of dope addiction ever captured on TV: Zendaya is all desperate agony and narcissistic shame as she unleashes her barrage of scathing insults.

“The Theater and Its Double” (Season 2, Episode 7)

Although the penultimate episode of Season 2 doesn’t have the same shock value as the bloody finale, it’s worth a shoutout because this is the part when Lexi Howard gets her revenge on all of her obnoxious and self-absorbed East Highland High friends. In a brilliant twist of “Hamlet”-esque meta-narrative, she puts on a school play where all the secret dramas and toxic conflicts of the school are revealed to everyone.

Related articles

  • Shows Like ‘Euphoria‘
  • 12 Margot Robbie Movies and Shows
  • 9 Jacob Elordi Movies and Shows

item.title: 9 Best Jacob Elordi Movies and TV Shows: Ranking His Most Iconic Roles
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Jacob Elordi has moved beyond heartthrob status. From “Saltburn” to his 2026 “Wuthering Heights” release, here are his 9 best movies and shows ranked.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Senior Staff Writer Randy Harward, our editorial lead with 25+ years covering TV, film, and music.Jacob Elordi has officially successfully shed his “Netflix boyfriend” image to become one of the most sought-after dramatic actors of his generation. We’ve watched him dominate the awards circuit recently for his transformative performance in Guillermo del Toro’s “Frankenstein,” defining a new era of dark, atmospheric dramas. Whether he’s playing a toxic high school athlete or a classic literary anti-hero, Elordi is currently the king of Prestige Mess—choosing projects that are guaranteed to be polarizing, visually stunning, and social-media-friendly.

1. “Frankenstein” (2025)

In a career-defining physical performance, Elordi starred as The Creature in Guillermo del Toro’s gothic masterpiece. The role required extensive prosthetics and saw Elordi winning Best Supporting Actor at the 2026 Critics’ Choice Awards. It’s a haunting, sympathetic portrayal that positions him as a serious heavyweight in the industry, and it’s hard to believe that the role originally went to Andrew Garfield, who dropped out due to scheduling conflicts.

Where to watch “Frankenstein”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Frankenstein” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

2. “Euphoria” (2019–2026)

After years of production delays, we are finally seeing the return of Nate Jacobs in “Euphoria” Season 3 this April. The new season features a four-year time jump, moving the characters out of high school and into a messy, complicated adulthood. Elordi has hinted that Nate’s storyline this time around is “incredibly liberating” and “singular,” suggesting he may be more isolated from the main ensemble than ever before.

Where to watch “Euphoria”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Euphoria” on HBO Max, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

3. “Wuthering Heights” (2026)

Set for a wide Valentine’s Day weekend release, this Emerald Fennell-directed adaptation is anything but a safe period piece. Starring alongside Margot Robbie as Heathcliff and Cathy, the film adopts a psychedelic, erotic, and highly stylized tone. While some book purists have debated the casting, the online elite are already praising the film’s prestige-pulp energy.

Where to watch “Wuthering Heights”

At the time of writing, “Wuthering Heights” is available to rent from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

4. “Priscilla” (2023)

Elordi took on the monumental task of playing Elvis Presley in Sofia Coppola’s intimate biopic, focusing on the perspective of Priscilla Presley. Unlike other versions of the King, Elordi’s performance highlights the darker, more controlling aspects of the icon behind closed doors. The actor’s towering height and low register brought a unique, brooding presence to the role that captivated critics.

Where to watch “Priscilla”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Priscilla” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

5. “Saltburn” (2023)

In this polarizing rich-kids-behaving-badly thriller, Elordi plays Felix Catton, the charismatic and wealthy student who becomes the object of obsession. The film cemented his status as a muse for high-concept directors like Emerald Fennell. It’s a visually stunning performance that relies heavily on Elordi’s natural magnetism and effortless cool.

Where to watch “Saltburn”

At the time of writing, you can rent “Saltburn” from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

6. “The Kissing Booth” (2018)

The film that started it all. While Elordi has since moved on to much darker fare, we can’t ignore the cultural impact of Noah Flynn. This Netflix original made him an overnight global heartthrob and the quintessential “Netflix boyfriend.” It serves as a fascinating time capsule of his early career before he pivoted to the unsettling roles he prefers today.

Where to watch “The Kissing Booth”

At the time of writing, you can watch “The Kissing Booth” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

7. “The Sweet East” (2023)

Elordi showed off his indie sensibilities in this picaresque journey through the fringes of American society. Playing Ian, a high-strung actor, he proved he could handle satirical, offbeat dialogue just as well as intense drama. It’s a small but memorable role that signaled his desire to work with experimental filmmakers.

Where to watch “The Sweet East”

At the time of writing, you can watch “The Sweet East” on Hulu, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

8. “Deep Water” (2022)

In this psychological thriller starring Ben Affleck and Ana de Armas, Elordi plays a young piano teacher who gets caught in the middle of a deadly marital game. While his screen time is limited, his character’s fate is a pivotal turning point in the film’s dark narrative. It was one of the first signs that Elordi was interested in the prestige pulp genre.

Where to watch “Deep Water”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Deep Water” on Hulu, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

9. “On Swift Horses” (2024)

In this 1950s period drama, Elordi plays Julius, a Korean War veteran living a secret life. The film explores themes of luck, love, and the American dream through a mid-century lens. It’s a quiet, internal performance that showcases his ability to convey deep emotion with very few words.

Where to watch “On Swift Horses”

At the time of writing, you can watch “On Swift Horses” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

Frequently asked questions

Is Jacob Elordi in the new "Frankenstein" movie?

Yes! Jacob Elordi stars as The Creature in Guillermo del Toro's "Frankenstein," which was released in late 2025. He replaced Andrew Garfield in the role and has already won several major awards for his performance.

When does "Euphoria" Season 3 come out?

After several delays, "Euphoria" Season 3 is officially confirmed to premiere on HBO and Max on April 12, 2026. The season will feature a significant time jump, following the characters into their early twenties.

Who does Jacob Elordi play in "Wuthering Heights?"

Jacob Elordi plays the iconic role of Heathcliff in the 2026 adaptation of "Wuthering Heights," directed by Emerald Fennell. He stars alongside Margot Robbie, who plays Catherine "Cathy" Earnshaw.

What to read next

  • Best Shows Like ‘Euphoria‘
  • Returning Shows We’re Excited for in 2026
  • What to Watch on HBO Max Right Now
  • HBO’s ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 is Back
CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: Best Fiber Internet Providers: Prices, Speeds, and More
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Shopping for fiber optic internet? Wondering how much is fiber optic internet? Our best fiber internet guide breaks down the top fiber ISPs on the market today.
item.content:encoded:
Best overall
Editorial rating (4.00/5)
Google Fiber
Price:
$70.00–$150.00/mo.
Max download speed:
1,000–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Google Fiber
Jump to review
Google Fiber
Price:
$70.00–$150.00/mo.
Max download speed:
1,000–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Best for bundles
Editorial rating (3.80/5)
Verizon Fios
Price:
$49.99–$109.99/mo. (w/ Auto Pay)
Max download speed:
300–2,300 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Verizon
Jump to review
Verizon Fios
Price:
$49.99–$109.99/mo. (w/ Auto Pay)
Max download speed:
300–2,300 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Best in reliability
Editorial rating (3.30/5)
Quantum Fiber
Price:
$45.00–$165.00/mo.
Max download speed:
200–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Quantum Fiber
Jump to review
Quantum Fiber
Price:
$45.00–$165.00/mo.
Max download speed:
200–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Fastest
Editorial rating (3.70/5)
Ziply Fiber
Price:
$20.00–$900.00/mo.
Max download speed:
50–50,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Ziply
Jump to review
Ziply Fiber
Price:
$20.00–$900.00/mo.
Max download speed:
50–50,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Best no-contract internet
Editorial rating (3.70/5)
AT&T Fiber
Price:
$55.00–$155.00/mo.
Max download speed:
100–5,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor AT&T
Jump to review
AT&T Fiber
Price:
$55.00–$155.00/mo.
Max download speed:
100–5,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited

Is fiber optic internet worth it?

Out of all internet technology types, fiber-optic internet is the fastest and most reliable. Many East and West Coast folks have access to our top picks for the best fiber internet like Google Fiber, Verizon Fios or Quantum Fiber. (Google and Verizon also earned Best of the Best spots in our annual top internet providers guide.) But even if you’re not on the coasts, you can find fiber internet in many major US cities. If you’re looking for the absolute fastest fiber internet available, we recommend Verizon Fiosfor its competitive pricing, sign-up incentives, and high customer satisfaction scores. In terms of bang for your buck, we like AT&T for its no-contract plans, competitive pricing, and excellent speeds. We’ll go over all your options for the best fiber internet providers below.

Why should you trust us? At CableTV.com, our internet experts have spent thousands of hours testing all of the best (and worst) internet services, and hundreds more hours tracking and researching industry trends. For this guide, we drew from our existing fiber internet reviews and coverage of major providers like Verizon, AT&T, and Google Fiber. As part of these reviews, we surveyed thousands of real fiber internet customers to see how they felt about their internet service and analyzed speed test data from our partners at HighSpeedInternet.com. We also combined this data with our original research, hands-on testing, and reporting to see which internet provider comes out on top.
Jump to:
  • Head-to-head comparison
  • Other top fiber optic internet providers
  • What to look for

Get faster internet today

Enter your zip code to see if fiber internet is available in your area.

Compare best fiber internet providers

Provider Price Download speeds Data limit
Google Fiber $70.00–$150.00/mo. 1,000–8,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Google Fiber

Read Google Fiber review
Verizon Fios $49.99–$109.99/mo. (w/ Auto Pay) 300–2,300 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Verizon

Read Verizon Fios review
Quantum Fiber $45.00–$165.00/mo. 200–8,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Quantum Fiber

Read Quantum Fiber review
Ziply Fiber $20.00–$900.00/mo. 50–50,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Ziply

Read Ziply review
AT&T Fiber $55.00–$155.00/mo. 100–5,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor AT&T

Read AT&T Fiber review

Data effective as of publish date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

Since we’re talking about fiber internet, pretty much every option is a great option. If our top picks for fiber-optic internet providers aren’t in your area, consider other fiber providers like CenturyLink or Frontier Fiber. Also, cable internet providers have fast download speeds, and they’re often more readily available than fiber optic internet networks.

Best overall: Google Fiber

Editorial rating (4/5)
Price:
$70.00–$150.00/mo.
Max download speeds:
1,000–8,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Google Fiber
Read full review

  • Simple plans and pricing
  • No contracts
  • Limited availability
Google Fiber was one of the top performers in our 2025 internet customer satisfaction survey and Best of the Best Awards for good reason. Google Fiber has excellent pricing, no data caps, and the fast speeds that only fiber internet can give you. Google Fiber also came out squarely on top for best customer support as the only fiber ISP to hit over 80% approval and under 5% disapproval ratings. Not too shabby. In testing data from our partners at HighSpeedInternet.com, Google Fiber had a middling latency time of 23.9 milliseconds. This’ll be fine for most households, but it’s a step behind fiber competitors like Verizon Fios and Frontier Fiber, who clocked in at around 10 milliseconds. But at the same time, customers are more than satisfied with Google Fiber’s performance—more than 80% of respondents approved of Google’s reliability and speed.

Google Fiber is the most honest and trustworthy and reliable provider I have ever I used since Comcast. Google Fiber is right there 24/7 if you ever need anything with your account.

Jed R., Google Fiber customer in Utah

Google also led the pack among all providers on pricing. The ISP isn’t necessarily cheap, as its starting plan is a 1 Gbps package that goes for $70.00/mo. But it’s one of the most affordable 1 Gbps plans among fiber ISPs, and customers know that they’re getting a deal. 80% of Google Fiber respondents were highly satisfied with Google Fiber’s prices, which led among all ISPs. Unfortunately, Google Fiber is hindered by its small footprint, as it’s currently available in fewer than 50 US cities. If you’re lucky enough to have their service near you, we seriously recommend you check them out.

Test Score (overall finish)
Average latency 23.9 milliseconds (15th)
Internet reliability approval rating 83% approval (4th)
Internet speed approval rating 83% approval (4th)
Price approval rating 80% approval (1st)
View Plansfor Google Fiber

Best for bundles: Verizon Fios

Editorial rating (3.8/5)
Price:
$49.99–$109.99/mo. (w/ Auto Pay)
Max download speeds:
300–2,300 Mbps
Data caps:
None
View Plansfor Verizon
Read full review

  • High customer satisfaction
  • Sign-up incentives
  • Limited availability

Verizon’s one of the few fiber internet providers that offers bundle services alongside internet, so they’ll be a great fit if you need to get coverage for an entire family. Between Verizon TV plans, home phone lines, and cell phone coverage, you can get bundle savings and have the convenience of multiple services on a single bill. When we surveyed over 7,000 people about their internet service, Verizon Fios customers were also among the most satisfied by far. Plus, Verizon Fios had multiple top-five polling finishes in internet speed, reliability, customer support, and monthly bill price. If Verizon Fios is available in your area, we say go for it. Its price range fits comfortably in most budgets, and Verizon Fios regularly woos new users with extra deals like free hardware and streaming service subscriptions. We certainly wouldn’t turn down the chance to binge-watch more TV with our internet service.

Test Score (overall finish)
Average latency 9.5 milliseconds (2nd)
Internet reliability approval rating 85% approval (2nd)
Internet speed approval rating 85% approval (2nd)
Price approval rating 66% approval (13th)
View Plansfor Verizon
I am message box. Click edit button to change this text.

Best in internet performance: Quantum Fiber

Editorial rating (3.3/5)
Price:
$45.00–$165.00/mo.**
Max download speed:
200–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Quantum Fiber
Read full review

  • Excellent internet performance
  • No contracts
  • No budget internet plan
  • Minimal bundle options

On one hand, Quantum Fiber was previously just CenturyLink fiber internet under a new name—former parent company Lumen sold the company to AT&T in 2026, though Quantum is operating (for now) as an independent brand. Corporate name-changing aside, Quantum still offers remarkably solid fiber internet service. The provider did respectable in our customer satisfaction survey polling. However, Quantum doesn’t offer much besides internet service—you’ll need to go elsewhere for TV coverage, and its $45.00–$50.00/mo. starting pricing hits the bare minimum for budget-minded households. But Quantum’s excellent service makes it a stellar option for eligible fiber internet customers.

Test Score (overall finish)
Average latency 4.05 milliseconds*(N/A)
Internet reliability approval rating 79% approval (7th)
Internet speed approval rating 88% approval (1st)
Price approval rating 74% approval (5th)

Average Quantum Fiber latency data calculated from FCC Broadband Consumer Labels. 

View Plansfor Quantum Fiber

Fastest: Ziply

Editorial rating (3.7/5)
Price:
$20.00–$900.00/mo.
Max download speeds:
50–50,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Ziply
Read full review

  • No contracts
  • Fastest download and upload speeds around
  • Limited availability

If you want the fastest gig internet on the market, then you’re gonna want Ziply Fiber internet. Even though the average customer won’t need the insanely high speeds (and equally high price) of their $900 per month 50 Gig plan, Ziply offers plenty of other fiber plans to fit your needs from 100 Mbps to 50,000 Mbps. Plus, Ziply’s fiber network has stellar latency times that’ll ensure you’ll have smooth online gaming and video calls. Plus, Ziply doesn’t have annual contracts, so you’re free to leave and switch providers with no extra fees. The downside is Ziply’s limited availability—their plans are only available in Idaho, Montana, Oregon, and Washington.

Test Score (overall finish)
Average latency 10.9 milliseconds (3rd)
Internet reliability approval rating 81% approval (5th)
Internet speed approval rating 82% approval (7th)
Price approval rating 63% approval (15th)
View Plansfor Ziply

Best no-contract service: AT&T Fiber

Editorial rating (4.1/5)
Price:
$55.00–$155.00/mo.
Max download speeds:
100–5,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor AT&T
Read full review

  • Speeds up to 5,000 Mbps
  • Great customer satisfaction
  • Middling pricing
  • Availability is still a work in progress

AT&T Fiber scored in the top five overall for multiple internet customer satisfaction survey categories and features no annual contracts. So, why isn’t AT&T Fiber our pick for the top fiber ISP? It’s a hair pricier than the competition, as alternatives like Frontier and Google Fiber are cheaper on a per-Gbps basis. Although AT&T is one of the few ISPs with a nationwide fiber footprint, its availability still varies heavily by city. Still, AT&T Fiber offers great performance for a fiber ISP. If you’re one of the lucky ones to have it in your area, we say sign up for it today.

Test Score (overall finish)
Average latency 17.1 milliseconds (10th)
Internet reliability approval rating 82% approval (3rd)
Internet speed approval rating 83% approval (3rd)
Price approval rating 77% approval (3rd)
View Plansfor AT&T

Other top fiber internet providers

Frontier

Editorial rating (3/5)
Price:
$29.99–$109.99/mo.
Max download speeds:
200–7,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Frontier
Read full review

Frontier Fiber, Frontier’s fiber-optic internet service, offers superfast download speeds at affordable rates. It has limited availability in 23 states, but if you live in a Frontier area, we recommend you grab it if you’re able. Frontier sometimes offers sign-up deals, so be sure to keep a lookout for those when signing up.

View Plansfor Frontier

MetroNet

Editorial rating (3.3/5)
Price:
$34.95–$109.95/mo.
Max download speeds:
150–5,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor MetroNet
Read full review

Another fiber ISP with limited availability, MetroNet is only available in 17 US states. Even so, MetroNet users also found a lot to like about its service. MetroNet came in top 10 in our 2025 internet customer satisfaction survey, beating out larger competitors like Spectrum, and CenturyLink. MetroNet also has super affordable fiber plans, with download and upload speeds between 100 Mbps and 5,000 Mbps, starting at just $34.95 per month.

View Plansfor MetroNet

CenturyLink

Editorial rating (3.1/5)
Price:
$50.00–$75.00/mo.
Max download speeds:
100–940 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor CenturyLink
Read full review

As mentioned earlier, fiber internet providers vary a lot by area, and sometimes you just have to take what you can get. If your area still has CenturyLink fiber, the provider fills in gaps left behind by other providers by offering fiber internet service in cities dotted throughout the US, from Orlando, Florida to Spokane, Washington. CenturyLink offers three fiber internet plans, and the 940 Mbps plan’s $70 monthly cost is one of the cheapest gigabit plans in the US. That’s a pretty killer deal compared to other providers’ gigabit plans that can run up to $100 a month. So don’t feel too bad if CenturyLink is your only fiber internet provider. It might not have earned a gold medal here, but it still has the zooming speeds and unbeatable reliability you turn to fiber internet for.

View Plansfor CenturyLink

WOW! fiber internet

Customer rating (4.5/5)
Price:
$30.00–$185.00/mo.
Max download speeds:
50–1,200 Mbps
Data caps:
1.5–3 TB
View Plansfor WOW!
Read full review

WOW! has solid fiber internet choices if you’re looking for an internet plan with no strings attached—but that’s only if it’s available near you. Unfortunately, WOW! is only available in nine states in the eastern half of the US, and its fiber internet rollout is even more limited. As of this writing, it’s only available in 15 cities total between Florida, Michigan, and South Carolina. If you’re lucky enough to live in a WOW!-serviced area, their fast, inexpensive internet plans are solid choices, especially with their lack of pesky contracts. WOW’s fiber plan options are even more limited, though, so keep that in mind.

View Plansfor WOW!

Optimum

Editorial rating (3.7/5)
Price:
$25.00–$90.00/mo.**
Max download speeds:
300–8,000 Mbps
Data caps:
Unlimited
View Plansfor Optimum
Read full review
Optimum is a relatively lower-profile contender on the fiber scene. But Optimum builds up its hype by offering flash deals. Sign up at the right time and you can snag massive discounts. Couple Optimum’s low cost with the fact that it offers sleek equipment and built-in fiber-optic Wi-Fi, and you can see why we like Optimum. Keep in mind, however, that our survey revealed that the Optimum customers aren’t among the most satisfied internet customers. We suspect that our Optimum participants may be in Optimum’s cable internet areas, which don’t have quite the same reliability or upload speeds. But if you’ve got Optimum fiber internet in your area, keep checking Optimum’s website for savings and then jump on those reduced prices.
View Plansfor CenturyLink

What to look for in a fiber internet provider

When it comes to fiber internet, you should look for the same things you would with any other type of internet provider:

  • Speeds that match your needs
  • Affordable prices
  • High customer satisfaction

Speed

Most fiber internet options come with speeds that comfortably cover today’s internet needs. Download speeds around 100 Mbps will make streaming a breeze, and faster speeds are great for families whose members use the internet at the same time. Keep in mind that upload speeds are also important, especially if you video chat, play online games, or put big files up online. As a general rule, fiber plans have higher upload speeds than cable, DSL, or satellite internet because they can support equal upload and download speeds.How fast is gig speed internet? With one gig, you can download an entire two-hour HD movie in about 32 seconds. That’s just enough time to adjust your pillow and blanket so you’re ready for a cozy night in.

Price

How much should your fiber optic internet plan cost? While prices vary from provider to provider, there are a couple of benchmarks to keep in mind:

  • 300 Mbps plans average just under $50/mo.
  • 940 Mbps or 1 gig plans tend to be just under $80/mo.

And be sure to see how long you can keep the price you signed up with. Some regional fiber ISPs offer lower promotional prices for only a year, though most guarantee prices for years and avoid tricks like 12-month price increases. You’ll also want to consider equipment costs. Some plans include a modem/router in the pricing, while others require you to lease them for about $10 per month. That extra fee can be a real pain if you’re not prepared for it.

Is fiber internet worth it?

Fiber providers offer the most advanced internet service available today. In our opinion, we think the best of the best is Google Fiber because of its competitive pricing, free goodies, and high customer satisfaction. We also highly recommend Optimum, AT&T Fiber, Ziply, and Verizon Fios if they’re available in your area. Check your zip code below to see if one of the providers we suggested is available in your neighborhood.

Best fiber internet providers FAQ

Our methodology

We researched every major fiber internet provider and ranked them according to price, speed, and customer satisfaction scores from our annual survey. We also looked at hidden fees, contracts, and sign-up incentives to bring you the best fiber internet providers out there. For more information about our methodologies, feel free to peruse our How We Rank page.

Related articles

  • Best Internet Providers
  • Best No-Contract Internet Providers
  • Best Cheap Internet Providers

item.title: Love the ‘XO, Kitty’ Cast? Here’s Where You Can Stream Their Past Work
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Love the XO, Kitty cast? We’ve researched where to stream their past work—from K-dramas to YouTube shorts—so you don’t have to. See the full guide now!
item.content:encoded:
With its third season launching April 2 on Netflix, “XO, Kitty” has become a certified streaming success. It should come as no surprise: The high school drama has a hit-making pedigree. Created by author Jenny Han—who also penned “The Summer I Turned Pretty” series—”Kitty” is a spin-off of the “To All The Boys I’ve Loved Before” books-turned-Netflix-movies, which were a smash in their own right when they were first released, essentially launching the careers of Noah Centineo and Lana Condor. But while “The Summer” and “To All The Boys” were set stateside, “XO, Kitty” is set at KISS, an international school in South Korea, where (we learn in season one) Lara Jean and Kitty Song Covey’s late mother matriculated in the late ’90s or early ’00s. Kitty goes to KISS to feel closer to the mom she lost and to her Korean roots, along the way accruing an international gang of buddies pulled from all over the globe. While the “XO, Kitty” cast was mostly unknown to American viewers prior to the show’s launch, that doesn’t mean its stars haven’t appeared in numerous projects, both in the states and worldwide. Here’s where you can stream the K-Dramas, American coming-of-age stories, and YouTube shorts starring the whole KISS crew.

Anna Cathcart (Katherine “Kitty” Song-Covey)

Though Anna Cathcart, who plays Kitty, has been living in the Song-Covey universe for over 8 years now, joining the “To All The Boys” cast when she was just 14, she actually had a fairly robust career as a child actor prior to that gig. At 12, she was cast as Agent Olympia in season two of PBS Kids’ hit series “Odd Squad,” anchoring each episode of that run, popping up in the show’s third season, and starring in both “Odd Squad: The Movie” and 20 online episodes of “Odd Tube.” All of Cathcart’s “Odd Squad” episodes, plus the movie, are available to stream on PBS Kids. They’re also available for rent or purchase on Prime Video and Fandango At Home, or Apple TV+. Before becoming Kitty, Cathcart also played Dizzy Tremaine, daughter of one of Cinderella’s evil step-sisters, in the Disney Channel Original Movie “Descendants 2.” That movie, as well as the animated sequel “Descendents: The Royal Wedding,” are available to stream on Disney+. That’s also where you’ll find “Spin,” another Disney Channel original movie starring Cathcart.

Gia Kim (Yuri Han)

Gia Kim was no stranger to stage and screen prior to landing “XO, Kitty”, having founded a theater company in Korea and worked as a reporter for Korea’s Arirang news channel. Still, “XO, Kitty” was Kim’s first commercial TV role and she hasn’t appeared in much else since landing the gig. IMDb says Kim is playing an unknown role in a “Untitled K-Pop Project” currently in post-production and also starring people like Tony Revolori (“Spider-Man”), Eric Nam (“The Traitors”), and Ji-Young Yoo (“K-Pop Demon Hunters”), but with little other information about the project out there online as of yet, Kim’s fans will just have to to wait and see where they’ll be able to watch that one.

Sang Heon Lee (Min-ho Moon)

Though he plays Kitty and Yuri’s dorm neighbor and sometime love interest on “XO, Kitty,” Sang Heon Lee is actually Kim’s real-life younger brother. The two attended the Island School in Hong Kong as kids before going off to develop their acting skills in college. “XO, Kitty” is Lee’s first big acting role, with his previous work mostly consisting of background gigs and reenactments in documentaries. Since landing “Kitty,” though, Lee has shown up in a few choice things, including 2023’s “Gran Turismo” movie, where he plays Joo-Hwan Lee. That film is available to stream on Hulu for free if you’re a subscriber, as well as on Fubo. You can also see Lee as the romantic lead in the Filipino TV series “Secret Ingredient,” which is about a Korean man who gives up his fancy life in Seoul in order to seek out a memorable childhood friend. That’s a little harder to find online than Kitty, but you can stream episodes either on YouTube or on SFlix should you be interested.

Choi Min-yeong (Dae-heon “Dae” Kim)

Though “XO, Kitty” fans know Choi Min-yeong as the show’s well-meaning one-time love interest, Dae, he’s actually been acting in Korean movies and TV shows for years. At just 12, he played Young Kang Tae-Joon in the hit K-Drama “The Promise,” which interested parties can stream on the KBS World YouTube channel. Several years later, Choi did a recurring role on high school coming of age series “Twenty-Five Twenty-One,” which was a huge smash in Korea and quickly became one of the highest-rated dramas in the county’s history. That show became so popular that in the US it’s actually available to stream on Netflix, a move that should let you know just how universal its appeal has become. Two of Choi’s other Korean series—the coming-of-age thriller “Weak Hero” and romantic drama “Itaewon Class”—are also available on Netflix in the states.

Anthony Keyvan (Quincy “Q” Shabazian)

Anthony Keyvan, who plays best friend and stellar athlete Q on “XO, Kitty”, has been acting professionally since he was just eight years old. Though a good portion of his early work is in short or student films, he’s done one-off guest roles in more than a few TV shows as well. Keyvan played “Young Sayid” in a 2009 episode of Lost, which is streaming on Hulu. He’s done episodes of “iCarly” (streaming on Paramount+), “Grey’s Anatomy” (Hulu), “In Plain Sight” (Netflix), “NCIS” (Paramount+) “Necessary Roughness” (Peacock), “Schooled” (Prime Video) and “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit” (Peacock). He’s also had multi-episode arcs on shows like “The Rookie” (Disney+), “Fresh Off The Boat” (Disney+), “Major Crimes” (Philo), “Generation” (Tubi), and the short-lived “Once Upon A Time In Wonderland” (Disney+), where he played a Young Jafar from “Aladdin.” And while his longest-running TV role has been as “XO, Kitty”‘s Q, Keyvan was also a series regular on both “Love, Victor” and “Alexa & Katie.” Both are series about high schools, with the latter being about a teen girl living with cancer while the former is a “Love, Simon” spin-off about a Latinx student coming out and coming to terms with his identity in Atlanta. “Alexa & Katie” is available to stream on Netflix, while “Love, Victor” is on Hulu.

Hojo Shin (Jiwon Ahn)

A South Korean-born actress who graduated from the University of Michigan’s prestigious School of Music Theater & Dance, Hojo Shin plays Jiwon Ahn on Netflix’s “XO, Kitty”. Prior to taking on that role, she appeared in a number of other TV series and movies, including “The Dead Girls Detective Agency,” a Snapchat Original streaming now on YouTube and on which Shin played “Kristen” for five episodes. Shin also appeared as Mia in the TV pilot “Good People,” which was penned by Lee Daniels and Whitney Cummings but unfortunately not picked up by Prime Video. Shin also played Janine in the short “Lily Chan & the Doom Girls” (YouTube), and someone intriguingly named Dance Squad Julia in the movie “Boy Genius” (Prime Video). Interestingly, Shin also voices the character of Echo in the video game “Overwatch,” which was released in 2016 on the PlayStation, Windows, and Xbox One platforms and in 2019 for the Nintendo Switch.

“XO, Kitty” cast FAQ

Where can I watch "XO, Kitty"?

"XO, Kitty" is available to stream on Netflix.

When does the third season of "XO, Kitty" come out?

The third season of "XO, Kitty" is set to be released on April 2, 2026. The show has not yet been officially renewed for a fourth season.

Which two "XO, Kitty" stars are related?

Sang Heon Lee and Gia Kim—who play Min-ho and Yuri, respectively—are actually brother and sister in real life.

How old are the actors in "XO, Kitty"?

Anna Cathcart, who plays Kitty, is 22. Sang Heon Lee is 29. Gia Kim is 33. Anthony Keyvan is 25.

item.title: Don’t Miss These 5 New Shows and Movies on Netflix This April
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: We researched every new show on Netflix in April 2026 and picked five so you don’t have to. See our expert staff picks.
item.content:encoded:
Whether you’re in the mood for a Mission: Impossible marathon, the complete Madagascar saga, or a brand-new Netflix Original, this month has something for you.

Below are some staff recs arriving on Netflix in April 2026. Check out the trailers and see if one (or more) is calling you.

5 CableTV.com staff picks for Netflix April 2026

1. “Big Mistakes” (2026)

If you’re a “Schitt’s Creek” fan, this comedy thriller from Dan Levy and Rachel Sennott is a no-brainer. The pair play siblings who get sucked into a world of blackmail, kidnapping, and organized crime. Sounds like they’re really up it, if ya follow me. —Randy Harward, Entertainment Editor

2. “Love on the Spectrum” Season 4 (2026)

This show, out of all the romance reality shows, is the real deal and the only one I’ll watch. ‘Nuff said? Okay, I’ll elaborate. It’s all about making a love connection in a world that isn’t built for you, which is just so relatable, even if you’re not neurodivergent. —Randy Harward, Entertainment Editor

3. “Stranger Things: Tales From ’85” (2026)

A bonus for all you Strangers (or Strangies or whatever you call yourselves) out there who may or may not have been disappointed by the ending of Season 5. As Contributing Writer Ian McLaren put it so succinctly, “The spinoff captures the vibe of classic Saturday-morning cartoons, but with the signature ‘Stranger Things’ dread.” I, for one, am excited to catch “Stranger Things: Tales From ’85” in the actual theater with my daughter. —Mike Strayer, Managing Editor

4. “This Is a Gardening Show” (2026)

If that trailer didn’t convince you to tune in to Zach Galifianakis’ latest foray into interview-style improv comedy, I don’t know what will (certainly not my words). Anyway, here I go, take “Between Two Ferns With Zach Galifianakis” and plant it in a greenhouse with everyone from enthusiastic children to bookish experts popping through, and you’ve got “This Is a Gardening Show.” Did I mention it looks very funny and heartfelt? —Mike Strayer, Managing Editor

5. “XO, Kitty” (2026)

Jenny Han’s streaming sensation XO, Kitty returns April 2 for an emotional third season. This To All the Boys spinoff follows Kitty’s final year at KISS, balancing a high-school bucket list with her complex feelings for Min Ho. Featuring a special appearance by Lana Condor, it’s a perfect blend of K-drama hijinks and heartfelt closure as graduation looms for its audience. —Randy Harward, Entertainment Editor

Looking for more new shows and movies on Netflix? Check out our comprehensive guide that we update monthly.

item.title: CableTV.com Recs: Don’t Miss These New Shows and Movies on Hulu This April
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: We researched every new show on Hulu in April 2026 and picked five so you don’t have to. See our expert staff picks.
item.content:encoded:
In the mood for a Shrek marathon or a complete Hunger Games saga binge? Hulu’s got those in April, plus a Hulu Original and more.

To get you started on your Hulu hunt, here are a handful of staff recommendations we think you’ll enjoy.

5 CableTV.com staff picks for Hulu April 2026

1. “Good Boy” (2025)

Don’t we all think our dogs (or cats) are movie stars? Ben Leonberg cast his own dog, Indy, as the star in this inventive horror film, which uses 100+ year-old editing techniques to convey the dog’s emotions. It’s surprisingly cool to experience supernatural horror through a dog’s eyes, not to mention effective and refreshing. “Good Boy” is a whole new spin on horror, and I hope to see a sequel someday. —Randy Harward, Entertainment Editor

2. “Napoleon Dynamite” (2004)

Napoleon and Pedro are back on Hulu, and I’m excited to see how the cult classic holds up (been ’bout 10 years since my last watch). If you’ve never basked in the crestfallen football glory of Uncle Rico or craved pocket tots, you gotta see this quirky comedy, especially if you call the Intermountain West home. —Mike Strayer, Managing Editor

3. “Nikki Glaser: Good Girl” (2026)

Well, we already recommended “Good Boy,” so what about one for the girls? (I know, cheap segue.) Don’t worry, Nikki Glaser has got you. Even if you don’t follow Glaser, chances are you’ve seen clips on social media or her hosting the Golden Globes live and in person. Her playful roasts at the Globes are what hooked me, so I’ll be looking forward to “Good Girl” later this month. —Mike Strayer, Managing Editor

4. “Paradise” (2025–present)

This isn’t an April release, but the second season wraps today (March 30). I’m losing sleep bingeing this smart sci-fi thriller, which is rife with personal, political, and post-apocalyptic drama and current-events-flavored existential dread. I highly recommend checking out “Paradise,” the tale of a Secret Service agent (Sterling K. Brown) who finds the President dead one morning. And that’s just the crest of the suspense tsunami. —Randy Harward, Entertainment Editor

5. “Sirāt” (2025)

Although it didn’t end up winning the Oscar for Best International Feature, “Sirāt” is well worth your time. Pedro Almodóvar (“All About My Mother” and “The Skin I Live In”) produced the film, and it’s truly an international affair, filmed in Morocco with primarily French and Spanish actors and crew. I’m not a raver or burner, but this thriller will have you “unce unce-ing” from beginning to end. —Mike Strayer, Managing Editor

Looking for more new shows and movies on Hulu? Check out our comprehensive guide that we update monthly.

item.title: Classic ‘Sesame Street’ Episodes Find Another Home, and You Can Watch Them for Free
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Tubi’s on a mission to carry more kids’ shows, and its latest acquisition is ‘Sesame Street.’ Find out more about the free streaming service and how to watch without a subscription.
item.content:encoded:
“Sesame Street” makes its FAST debut

The full “Sesame Street” library is currently split between HBO Max and Netflix, two of the most expensive streaming services on the market. But you also have some free options, like PBS Kids and YouTube. Starting this week, you’ll have one more option for watching free “Sesame Street” episodes. Select episodes from the first 38 seasons are now available to stream on the free, ad-supported streaming television (FAST) service Tubi. Looking for the full rundown on how to watch “Sesame Street” in 2026? Head over to our comprehensive watch guide. For more info on the episodes coming to Tubi, read on.

Tubi is a great place to find kids shows

Tubi has been making a splash in the family entertainment world lately. After HBO Max systematically removed years’ worth of Cartoon Network shows from its platform, Tubi swooped in to save them from becoming lost media. You can now watch dozens of classic cartoons for free, including “The Powerpuff Girls,” “Foster’s Home for Imaginary Friends,” and “Dexter’s Laboratory.” According to Tubi’s social media posts, you can expect even more titles to appear on the platform throughout 2026. Tubi is in its, and I quote, “cartoon era.” And while “Sesame Street” is performed live with puppets, it seems that it’s become part of that new, kid-friendly vision that Tubi has for its revamped library. It makes sense, too: While Tubi isn’t owned by Warner Bros. Discovery, it does tend to pick up all of the IPs that WBD throws away.

How to find “Sesame Street” on Tubi

”Sesame Street” lives under the “Family Shows” genre on Tubi, alongside titles like “The Flintstones,” “Barney,” and “Diff’rent Strokes.” Or you could jump right to the “Sesame Street” page by clicking here. You’ll find classic episodes from the show’s first 38 seasons (up to 2007), including the 1969 pilot “Gordon Introduces Sally to Sesame Street.”

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • ‘Sesame Street’ Partners with Netflix and YouTube
  • How to Watch ‘Sesame Street’
  • Tubi Saves Classic Cartoon Network Shows

item.title: Sesame Street Isn’t Gone—Here’s How to Get There
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Find every way to stream Sesame Street, from new episodes to classic seasons. Our guide shows you where to find free content and helps you decide the best platform based on your budget.
item.content:encoded:
Wondering where Sesame Street is?

”Sesame Street” has been in the news a lot lately as it briefly lost and regained funding. But have no fear—it’s back on the air, and it’s easy to find new episodes. Sesame Street has been delighting kids and their families since 1969. When I was a kid, there was one way to watch it: turn the TV to channel 11 and hope it was on. (Even if it wasn’t, there was usually “Arthur”, “Dragon Tales”, or “Cyberchase”, so I was fine regardless.) But kids today have a lot more options. You can watch most episodes on demand, whenever you want—if you know where to look. I’d recommend different services based on the kind of “Sesame Street” content you’d like to watch. So scroll down until you find the heading that best matches your situation! I’ll do my best to be brief so you can get right to watching the show.

Looking for the hottest entertainment?

Subscribe to our email newsletter to get the latest TV premieres, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

I want to watch new “Sesame Street” episodes for free!

PBS Kids is your best bet! You can watch dozens of the latest “Sesame Street” episodes on the PBS Kids website. The episodes are free to watch with limited ads from PBS’s sponsors, like Google and the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. They’re great if you just want to sit down and watch some new episodes on any device, without having to log in to anything. You can also play lots of fun Sesame Street games, like Supermarket Challenge, Field Day Fun, and Nature Explorers.

Watch on PBS Kids
Tubi is another great option. This free, ad-supported streaming television (FAST) service added select “Sesame Street” episodes to its library in April 2026, and focuses on historical milestones from the show’s earlier seasons. If you’re looking to show your kid what things were like when you were their age, this is the way to go.
Watch on Tubi
If you’d like more free “Sesame Street” content, there are certain clips and full episodes on “Sesame Street”’s YouTube channel. We “love” how much “Sesame Street” is available to the public for free, but parents, remember to supervise your kids if they’re watching content on YouTube. The content there isn’t vetted the way kids’ sections on streaming services like Netflix are. Even on YouTube Kids, it’s easy to fall down a rabbit hole of videos and end up with content you weren’t expecting. I know it can be tempting to pop YouTube on and walk away—but if you need some distraction-free entertainment quickly, I’d recommend PBS Kids, Tubi, or a traditional streaming service before YouTube. Make YouTube something you and your kids watch together!

I want to watch “Sesame Street” live on my TV!

You’ll want to turn your TV to PBS. PBS comes with pretty much all cable and satellite TV plans, or you could watch it with an antenna. You also have access to PBS if you have YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, or live TV streaming with DIRECTV. (Our experts’ favorite live TV option is YouTube TV, but feel free to look around and find the service that works for you.) Depending on where you live and what service you’re using, PBS will be a different station. We’ve compiled a list of some of the biggest U.S. cities if you’d like to check for PBS’s channel number in your location. (We know it’s a big chart! Kids, if you have trouble reading it, ask a parent or guardian for help.)”Sesame Street” airs during the PBS Kids block in the morning—usually around 10 a.m. ET. But if you miss watching it live, you still have a lot of other options.

I want to watch new “Sesame Street” episodes on demand!

If you just want to play new “Sesame Street” episodes on a regular streaming service, you’ll want Netflix. Netflix carries every new “Sesame Street” episode beginning with Season 56. “Sesame Street” is rated TV-Y, so it should be available on all profiles unless you’ve blocked the title specifically.

I want to watch old “Sesame Street” episodes on demand!

HBO Max owned the streaming rights to new “Sesame Street” episodes before Netflix bought them. That means that HBO Max is still streaming old episodes from seasons 1–55. If things go as planned, these episodes will stay on HBO Max through 2027. So you don’t have to worry about them disappearing for the time being! When they finally expire, we expect they’ll end up on Netflix, too. You can also find select episodes from the first 38 seasons on the FAST service Tubi, no login required.

“Sesame Street” FAQ

Is "Sesame Street" on any streaming service?

Yes, you can watch old "Sesame Street" episodes on HBO Max and Tubi, and new "Sesame Street" episodes on Netflix.

Where can I watch all "Sesame Street" episodes?

HBO Max still has the rights to all old "Sesame Street" episodes through 2027. It carries seasons 1–55, but seasons 56 and beyond will be on Netflix. You can also watch select episodes and clips on the "Sesame Street" YouTube channel.

Will "Sesame Street" still be free?

Yes, "Sesame Street" is still free to watch, even though it's also on Netflix. You can find free episodes on the PBS Kids website and app, or by tuning into PBS Kids on your TV.

Who we are

Here at CableTV.com, we’re committed to answering the tough questions. With the rise of so many competing streaming services and TV packages, it can be hard to know where to find your favorite shows from yesteryear. That’s where we come in: Our team of experts is here to guide you straight to the answers you need. So you can get back to relaxing in front of your TV.

Related articles

  • Sesame Street Partners with Netflix and YouTube
  • Does Netflix Have Movies for Kids?
  • The Best Kids’ Christmas Movies

item.title: 9 Best Zendaya Movies and TV Shows: Ranking Her Most Memorable Roles
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Catch up on Zendaya movies and TV shows before Euphoria Season 3 drops in April 2026! Our experts ranked her 9 best roles and tell you where to stream them.
item.content:encoded:
Alongside fellow actors Timothée Chalamet and Sydney Sweeney, the mononymous superstar Zendaya is defining the Gen Z era of Hollywood with a versatile style that’s somehow simultaneously down-to-earth, full of confidence, and kinda weird. Born in 1996, Zendaya got her start on the Disney Channel but now she’s in basically every TV and movie theater in America, acting alongside other huge stars and capturing the energy of the moment with her iconic film and TV roles. In time for the premiere of Season 3 of “Euphoria” (which premieres April 12 on HBO Max), we took a trip down memory lane to highlight the best roles that Zendaya has delivered to the zeitgeist thus far.

9. “Zapped” (2014)

In this forgettable Disney Channel Original Movie from 2014, Zendaya plays a high schooler who gets her hands on a phone app that can control the obnoxious boys and men around her. It’s a very pre-“Euphoria” role for the up-and-coming star, and a critic for the New York Times could tell that the movie was just a vehicle for her growing fame, providing “one tiny step in her impending ubiquity.”

Where to watch “Zapped”

You can stream “Zapped” on Disney+ or you can rent it from a video-on-demand platform like Prime Video or Apple TV.

8. “Smallfoot” (2018)

Zendaya had some of her first big roles in musicals, including this animated comedy about a family of Yetis in the Himalayas. Though she’s definitely a better actor than singer, it’s clear she’s having a blast in “Smallfoot” as the bright and plucky Meechee.

Where to watch “Smallfoot”

You can watch “Smallfoot” by renting it on a video-on-demand platform like Prime Video or Apple TV.

7. “Space Jam: A New Legacy” (2021)

Plenty of millennials think back fondly to the original “Space Jam” from 1996, and it makes sense that this sequel would bring in a certified Gen Z icon like Zendaya for this 2021 sequel. Here she voices the inimitable Lola Bunny alongside LeBron James.

Where to watch “Space Jam: A New Legacy”

You can watch “Space Jam: A New Legacy” by renting it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

6. “Shake It Up” (2010–2013)

Before Zendaya broke out as a 17-year-old drug-sniffing, grief-stricken wreck in “Euphoria,” she was starring as a clever 14-year-old full of optimism on this Disney Channel sitcom.

Where to watch “Shake It Up”

You can stream “Shake It Up” on Disney+ or you can buy it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

5. “Malcolm & Marie” (2021)

Directed by Sam Levinson (the same guy who created “Euphoria”), “Malcolm & Marie” got panned by a lot of critics for its overcooked tone and self-indulgent premise. But Zendaya has undeniable chemistry with co-star John David Washington in this dialogue-heavy drama about a creative couple figuring themselves out.

Where to watch “Malcolm & Marie”

You can stream “Malcolm & Marie” on Netflix or you can rent it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

4: “Challengers” (2024)

Zendaya has a real gift for standing out in complex romances and spicy dramas. That is most clear in “Challengers,” in which she exudes pure confidence and poise as an ex-tennis pro turned manager who helps kickstart a deliriously entertaining love triangle between herself, her tennis ball-whacking husband and a fellow competitor on the courts.

Where to watch “Challengers”

You can stream “Challengers” on Hulu or you can rent it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

3. “Spider-Man: No Way Home” (2021)

In one of her coolest roles, Zendaya appears as the socially awkward, down-to-earth Michelle “MJ” Jones-Watson in three Spider-Man movies—including this multiverse-warping epic in which she doesn’t even need superpowers to help save the day after Parker’s cover gets blown and a magic spell goes wrong. She’ll be back as MJ in “Spider-Man: Brand New Day,” which hits theaters on July 31, 2026.

Where to watch “Spider-Man: No Way Home”

You can watch “Spider-Man: No Way Home” on streaming platform STARZ or you can rent it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

2. “Dune: Part Two” (2024)

After appearing multiple times in Paul Atreides’ spice-addled visions and then emerging with the Fremen tribe in the first installment of “Dune,” Zendaya’s Chani takes on a central role in “Dune: Part Two.” She powerfully plays a fierce and skeptical foil to the messianic Paul, providing an emotional anchor for this highfalutin’ sci-fi fantasy in which she acts alongside her real-life bestie Timothée Chalamet.

Where to watch “Dune: Part Two”

You can stream “Dune: Part Two” on HBO Max or rent it from video-on-demand platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV.

1. “Euphoria” (2019–present)

Zendaya won two Emmy Awards for her lead role on “Euphoria,” in which she makes a radical break from her previous roles as a Disney tween idol. Zendaya stars as Rue Bennett, a sharp but damaged teenager facing down drug addiction and navigating other rite-of-passage struggles in a fictional California suburb.

Where to watch “Euphoria”

You can stream “Euphoria” on HBO Max or buy it from video-on-demand platform Apple TV.
